Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

I like this patch in general. 

Before it can land, we need to discuss what the interoperation issues are (if any?) with old servers and new clients that are trying to use the new unlink + close RPC. It looks like the case of old clients with new servers should be handled properly, just by the close handle not being sent with the unlink. 

It might be that this needs to use a new connect flag (= 0x100000000000000) from the client to detect if the MDS can handle unlink + close.

yes, it has interop issue with old server, I'll add a connect flag.

Patch Set 5:

It's a pretty standard procedure to piggyback additional operation in a close RPC, please take a look ll_close_inode_openhandle() -> mdc_close() and:

        if (op_data->op_bias & MDS_HSM_RELEASE) {
                req_fmt = &RQF_MDS_RELEASE_CLOSE;

In my opinion, it's not necessary to invent another mechanism for this and we can just extend it to have a unlink-on-close RPC.

It'll be not necessary to have connection flag (which we tend to invent new flags arbitrarily); if the server executed the unlink at close, it will set a flag in the reply RPC, so if it turned out the server is an old one, one extra unlink RPC will be issued followed by.

If you really like to adopt my suggestion, please keep in mind we already have release-on-close, and we're going to have swap-on-close and here we're having unlink-on-close extension here. It will be great if we can have same RPC format for all of them.

Please let me know if I understood the problem wrong.

Patch Set 5:

IMO this is different from the release-on-close case, because this is already in unlink context, it's strange to pack a close request but with unlink format, and why we not just do unlink with file handle packed?

BTW RQF_MDS_RELEASE_CLOSE is a simple extension of RQF_MDS_CLOSE, but RQF_MDS_REINT_UNLINK not, I can't think of a way to extend in a simple and straightforward way.

Patch Set 8:

Andreas, I don't quite get you. For close, it needs to store transno separately because it uses a different portal; but for close in unlink, it's part of unlink operation, so it doesn't have its own transno. Could you explains more about how it affects uncommitted open?

Patch Set 8: Verified-1

I don't think that the close needs its own transno, there should only be a single transno for the combined unlink and close RPCs.

I was just thinking that there may still be a deadlock with the uncommitted open and the close, but I think you are right that cannot happen if they are being sent on the same portal.


A separate issue is that this patch has always been failing review-dne-part-2 replay-single test_70b in every test run, even though sometimes Maloo gives it Verified +1 (due to TEI-1508).  Fortunately, the patch has not landed or it would have caused permanent failures of all tests.  Please see:

commit.subject: LU-4367 close: combine close in unlink RPC
commit.message:
LU-4367 close: combine close in unlink RPC

With open lock taken, close will cache file handle instead of
release it on MDS, but if unlink is followed by close, a real
close will be triggered before unlink, which will hurt unlink
performance.

This patch combines close into unlink RPC when SOM is disabled,
because current SOM implementation has flaws, and supporting it
will cause unexpected complexity. In the future if SOM is mainly
implemented on server side, this feature should coexist with it.

* client packs file handle in unlink reint record.
* MDT close file handle before unlinking.

Signed-off-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Change-Id: I7f4b3f5eae30183ac269ac50b8f196c7ab73c76d
